next_best_action: Investigate the GraphQL schema change in the recent deployment that caused CatalogItem.description to return null for non-nullable fields.
remediation_steps: Review the recent deployment changes to the graphql-gateway product catalog API
Check the CatalogItem schema definition for the description field nullability constraint
Verify the backing data source has populated description fields for all catalog items
Roll back the schema change or fix the null values in the catalog database
Confirm the procurement portal can browse and order equipment after the fix

next_best_action: Address the OOMKilled pod issue by increasing memory limits or reverting the batch size change from INC-3847 that is causing excessive memory consumption.
remediation_steps: Review the batch size change from INC-3847 that increased order processing batch size
Increase the pod memory limit or revert the batch size to the previous value
Monitor the order-processing pods for memory usage after the change
Verify Kafka consumer group rebalancing completes successfully after pod restart
Confirm order processing pipeline is functional and customer orders are flowing

next_best_action: Investigate the config service v2.4.0 upgrade that introduced double-escaped JSON serialization in configuration values.
remediation_steps: Review the config service v2.4.0 release notes for serialization changes
Check if the JSON serialization library was upgraded with double-encoding behavior
Fix the serialization logic to produce properly escaped JSON values
Verify billing, notification, and reporting services can deserialize configs correctly
Deploy the fix and restart affected downstream services

next_best_action: Resolve the webpack build failure caused by breaking changes in @contoso/ui-kit v5 export paths after the npm update.
remediation_steps: Check the @contoso/ui-kit v5 migration guide for updated import paths
Update import statements in EmployeeTable and Dashboard components
Resolve the CSS ordering conflict between grid.css and overrides.css
Pin the @contoso/ui-kit dependency to a compatible version if needed
Verify the build completes successfully and deploy the HR dashboard

next_best_action: Investigate the 409 Conflict error on Azure Web App settings update — likely caused by a deployment slot lock from the previous manual deployment.
remediation_steps: Check for deployment slot locks or swap operations on contoso-prod-webapp
Verify no manual deployment locks were left from the v3.7.9 portal deployment
Remove any stale locks preventing Set-AzWebApp app settings updates
Re-run the deployment script to confirm the 409 Conflict is resolved
Add lock cleanup to the deployment script to prevent future occurrences

next_best_action: Repair the Outlook profile and Teams sign-in state that were reset by Windows Update KB5034441.
remediation_steps: Check if KB5034441 is known to reset Office/Teams registry keys
Re-create the Outlook mail profile and reconfigure the Exchange account
Clear the Teams cache and re-sign in to restore the session
Verify Identity registry keys (EnableADAL, etc.) are set correctly
Monitor after next Windows Update cycle to confirm settings persist

next_best_action: Renew the expired Contoso Intermediate CA G2 certificate and redistribute it to all services connecting to the payment gateway.
remediation_steps: Confirm the Intermediate CA G2 certificate has expired
Generate and sign a new intermediate CA certificate from the Root CA
Deploy the renewed certificate to pay-gw.internal.contoso.com
Update the certificate trust chain on all client services (billing, order-processing, refunds)
Verify TLS handshakes complete successfully for all dependent services

next_best_action: Identify and kill the process occupying port 8080 on app-prod-03 so the contoso-webapp service can start.
remediation_steps: Run netstat or ss to identify the process occupying port 8080
Determine if a zombie process from the maintenance window is holding the port
Kill the stale process and restart the contoso-webapp systemd service
Verify the web application starts successfully and passes health checks
Add a pre-start check to the systemd unit to detect port conflicts

next_best_action: Investigate the Outlook character encoding issue for emails with mixed Chinese and English content, likely related to the recent Exchange server migration.
remediation_steps: Check the Exchange server migration logs for encoding configuration changes
Verify the Exchange transport rules preserve UTF-8 encoding for international content
Test sending mixed Chinese/English emails between migrated and non-migrated mailboxes
Check the Outlook client encoding detection settings and MIME charset headers
Apply encoding fixes to the Exchange server or Outlook client as needed

next_best_action: Investigate the 502 Bad Gateway errors from the nginx reverse proxy — the upstream backend at 10.20.30.41:8080 is timing out after 60 seconds.
remediation_steps: Check the application health on the upstream server 10.20.30.41:8080
Review application logs for slow queries or resource exhaustion
Increase the nginx upstream timeout if the backend legitimately needs more time
Check if the backend connection pool is saturated or the server is under load
Verify the expense reporting app is responsive after the fix

next_best_action: Investigate why forwarded emails from the vendor portal display raw MIME content instead of properly rendered attachments.
remediation_steps: Check the vendor portal email forwarding configuration for MIME handling
Verify the Exchange server correctly processes multipart/mixed forwarded messages
Test with different email clients to isolate whether the issue is server or client side
Check if a recent Exchange update changed MIME content-type handling
Apply the fix and confirm attachments render properly in forwarded vendor emails

next_best_action: Investigate the database migration error from PR #1247 (invoice-v2) that is causing the production deployment to fail.
remediation_steps: Review the migration scripts in commit 8e7f3b4 for schema conflicts
Check if the migration targets a table or column that was modified separately
Run the migration in a staging environment to reproduce the error
Fix the migration script or adjust the existing schema to be compatible
Re-deploy to production after verifying the migration succeeds

next_best_action: Grant the Synapse Contributor role to the Terraform service principal so it can create the Synapse Workspace in the data lake environment.
remediation_steps: Identify the service principal used by Terraform for the datalake provisioning
Request and assign the Synapse Contributor role on the target subscription
Verify the role assignment propagates (may take a few minutes)
Re-run terraform apply to complete the data lake environment provisioning
Confirm all 8 resources are created successfully

next_best_action: Investigate the internal DNS resolution failure for the contoso.internal zone — check SOA serial synchronization between primary and secondary DNS servers.
remediation_steps: Verify dns-prod-01 (primary) is serving the contoso.internal zone correctly
Check if dns-prod-02 (secondary) has a stale or desynchronized zone copy
Force a zone transfer from primary to secondary to update the SOA serial
Verify all internal hostnames (app-prod, db-prod, web-prod) resolve correctly
Monitor DNS query logs to confirm resolution is stable for all 500+ users

next_best_action: Investigate the intermittent packet loss between Seattle (10.10.x) and Portland (10.50.x) offices causing TCP retransmissions and connection resets.
remediation_steps: Check the WAN link and router interfaces between Seattle and Portland for errors
Review QoS policies and bandwidth utilization on the inter-site link
Run extended packet captures to identify the specific network segment with loss
Check for CRC errors or interface flapping on intermediate switches/routers
Engage the ISP or circuit provider if the issue is on the WAN link
